perturbation.original_text: 1.11 "Delivery" shall mean the time when the Product is placed at the disposal of Kitov at Dexcel's Facility based on an **** (Incoterms® 2010).
perturbation.changed_text: 1.11 "Delivery" shall mean the time when the Product is placed at the disposal of Kitov at Dexcel's Warehouse based on an EXW (Incoterms® 2010).
perturbation.explanation: The change from "Facility" to "Warehouse" and the change from "****" to "EXW" changes the location, impacting cost & responsibility for shipping. The original text uses Facility in the definition of the word 'Delivery', and using 'Warehouse' creates uncertainty about location for delivery.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3.8 Kitov shall be solely responsible, at its own cost and expense, for all activities related to the sale, marketing, shipping, distribution, storage following the delivery of the Products, order fulfilment, invoicing, collection, and any other activities directly or indirectly related to the promotion, marketing, distribution, or sale of the Product in any country.
perturbation.justification: The change in the definition of 'Delivery' from Dexcel's 'Facility' to Dexcel's 'Warehouse' and the change in Incoterms from '****' to 'EXW' directly contradicts section 3.3.8. Section 3.3.8 states that Kitov is responsible for shipping *following the delivery of the Products*. By changing the delivery location and Incoterms, the point at which Kitov's responsibility begins is altered. For example, 'EXW' (Ex Works) places more responsibility on the buyer (Kitov) from an earlier point. Therefore, the modification creates a conflict as to who is responsible for the shipping activities *following delivery*, as the very definition of delivery has been altered to shift these responsibilities. This fits the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1.1 Kitov shall provide Dexcel with reasonable Packaging and Labelling instructions for the Livery (by SKU), including, but not limited to, artwork for Labels and patient leaflets, as soon as practicable following the Effective Date; provided, however, that Kitov shall provide such instructions at least one hundred and twenty (120) days prior to the anticipated Supply Commencement Date. Kitov shall provide Dexcel with its Product Packaging and Labeling instructions, including, but not limited to, approved artwork, with respect to any new SKU (for a new Product Distributor or new country), as well as changes to or destruction of existing materials at least one hundred and fifty (150) days prior to the anticipated first supply of each such SKU.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1.1 Kitov shall provide Dexcel with reasonable Packaging and Labelling instructions for the Livery (by SKU), including, but not limited to, artwork for Labels and patient leaflets, as soon as practicable following the Effective Date; provided, however, that Kitov shall provide such instructions at least one hundred and twenty (120) days prior to the anticipated Supply Commencement Date. Kitov shall provide Dexcel with its Product Packing and Labeling instructions, including, but not limited to, approved artwork, with respect to any new SKU (for a new Product Retailer or new region), as well as changes to or destruction of existing materials at least one hundred and fifty (150) days prior to the anticipated first supply of each such SKU.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Packaging' to 'Packing', 'Distributor' to 'Retailer', and 'country' to 'region' creates ambiguity regarding the specific responsibilities and relationships, potentially conflicting with other sections that use the original terms. It is unclear whether 'Packing' has the same legal implications as 'Packaging,' or whether 'Retailer' falls under the definition of 'Distributor' elsewhere in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Kitov shall be responsible (itself or through its Affiliates and Distributors) for all costs related to the maintenance of or changes to the Specifications, materials, suppliers of the API and/or other materials used for the manufacture or Packaging of the Product, regulatory dossiers, and/or the Marketing Authorizations for the Product. Dexcel shall provide any and all reasonable assistance to Kitov in this respect during the Term.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Packaging' to 'Packing', 'Distributor' to 'Retailer', and 'country' to 'region' creates ambiguity and potential contradictions, because Section 2.2 specifies responsibilities for 'Packaging' and acts through 'Distributors'. If 'Packing' and 'Retailer' are interpreted as distinct from 'Packaging' and 'Distributor' respectively, then section 2.2 would no longer apply to them. This creates an in-text contradiction as the changes impact the scope of responsibilities outlined in Section 2.2, aligning with the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7.2 Kitov shall provide Dexcel with written notification of any shortfalls in shipment quantity, and (a) any out-of-specification temperature excursions based on the downloaded data logger information following compliance with the provisions of the Quality Agreement, and/or (b) any failure of the Product to meet the Specifications which are apparent upon visual inspection and/or identification testing of the Product delivered to it by Dexcel (each of (a) and (b) being an "Apparent Defect"), such notification to be provided within thirty (30) Working Days of receipt of the Product at Kitov's warehouse, accompanied by samples of any such allegedly defective Product and any such Product shall not be removed from quarantine until their status is resolved. In the event that a defect is not apparent upon visual inspection during the shelf life of the Product ("Hidden Defect"), Kitov shall use commercially reasonably best efforts to provide Dexcel with written notification within thirty (30) Working Days of discovering the same, to be accompanied by samples of any such allegedly defective Product, if such samples are available In the event of any failure by Kitov to provide Dexcel with written notification of any such shortfall, Apparent Defect or Hidden Defect within the respective aforementioned periods, it shall be deemed as Kitov having accepted the relevant consignment.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7.2 Kitov shall provide Dexcel with written notification of any shortfalls in shipment quantity, and (a) any out-of-specification temperature excursions based on the downloaded data logger information following compliance with the provisions of the Quality Agreement, and/or (b) any failure of the Product to meet the characteristics which are apparent upon visual inspection and/or identification testing of the Product delivered to it by Dexcel (each of (a) and (b) being an "Apparent Defect"), such notification to be provided within thirty (30) Working Days of receipt of the Product at Kitov's warehouse, accompanied by samples of any such allegedly defective Product and any such Product shall not be removed from quarantine until their status is resolved. In the event that a characteristic is not apparent upon visual inspection during the shelf life of the Product ("Latent Defect"), Kitov shall use commercially reasonably best efforts to provide Dexcel with written notification within thirty (30) Working Days of discovering the same, to be accompanied by samples of any such allegedly defective Product, if such samples are available In the event of any failure by Kitov to provide Dexcel with written notification of any such shortfall, Apparent Defect or Latent Defect within the respective aforementioned periods, it shall be deemed as Kitov having accepted the relevant consignment.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Specifications' to 'characteristics' and 'Hidden Defect' to 'Latent Defect' creates uncertainty. While seemingly synonymous, 'Specifications' is defined in Section 1.35, creating a specific standard, and 'characteristics' has a much broader, undefined meaning. Also, Latent Defect is not defined. The agreement mentions Hidden Defect to have the original meaning in 3.7.2, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7.3 Dexcel shall use its best efforts to make up any shortfall in shipment quantity as soon as practicable after being notified by Kitov of such shortfall. In the event of Product which Kitov claims have Apparent Defects or Hidden Defects, Dexcel shall have up to thirty (30) Working Days after receipt of the samples to show that the Product in question meets the Specifications ("Period"). In the event that no agreement is reached by the end of the Period, Kitov shall have the right to submit a new purchase order, which Dexcel shall satisfy as soon as possible using reasonable commercial efforts ("Replacement Shipment"), and Dexcel shall require proof that Kitov has destroyed that part or all of the original shipment with claimed defective Product. In the event that Kitov has fully paid the Supply Price for the claimed defective Product, Dexcel shall supply the Replacement Shipment at no additional Transfer Price. In the event that Kitov has not fully paid the Supply Price for the claimed defective Product, Kitov will pay for the Replacement Shipment in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (assuming the Replacement Shipment meets the Specifications).
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a contradiction. The original text defines 'Apparent Defects' and 'Hidden Defects' with reference to 'Specifications', which is itself defined in Section 1.35. The changed text replaces 'Specifications' with 'characteristics' and 'Hidden Defect' with 'Latent Defect'. 'Characteristics' is undefined and has a broader meaning than the defined 'Specifications', introducing ambiguity. The term 'Latent Defect' is also undefined, while the agreement originally defines 'Hidden Defect'. The contradicted text in 3.7.3 refers to 'Apparent Defects' and 'Hidden Defects' and states that Dexcel has up to thirty (30) Working Days after receipt of the samples to show that the Product in question meets the 'Specifications'. By changing 'Specifications' to 'characteristics' and 'Hidden Defect' to 'Latent Defect' in 3.7.2, the standard against which Dexcel must prove compliance becomes unclear, and it's now unclear what 'Latent Defect' means, thus contradicting the requirements outlined in 3.7.3. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
